QF.id: Clubbing, Set-Off and Carry Forward of Losses_5

question.text: Which of the following statements about carry forward of losses is CORRECT?

answer.text: Speculative business loss can be carried forward for 8 assessment years
Unabsorbed depreciation can be carried forward for a maximum of 8 assessment years
Unabsorbed depreciation can be carried forward for an unlimited number of years
Long-term capital loss can be carried forward for 4 assessment years

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Unabsorbed depreciation under Section 32(2) can be carried forward for an UNLIMITED number of years. This is unique — speculative business loss is 4 years, non-speculative business loss is 8 years, capital loss (both LTCL and STCL) is 8 years, and house property loss is 8 years. **The Trap:** Option A swaps the speculative (4 years) and non-speculative (8 years) carry forward periods. Option D applies the speculative period to capital losses. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series X-A covers carry forward periods at a basic level for financial planning. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Unabsorbed depreciation is the most powerful loss carry forward tool — no time limit and can be set off against any income except salary. Advisers should track this for business clients carefully.
